messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] multinomial logit models
messages.body_plain:
Dear R community,

Is anybody aware of how to do diagnostics for multinomial logit models?
In the binomial case (for GLM's), I have already used the functions 
provided by J. Fox in his car package.
I estimate my model with multinom (nnet).
Now I would like to do an analysis of the residuals, influence measures, 
nonlinearity and collinearity among the predictors.
How is generally controlled for the results in MNL using R?
Thank you very much in advance

Best regards,

Robert Glass

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching]  multinomial logit models (fwd)
messages.body_plain:
Dunno but this list is for people using R to teach INTRODUCTORY
statistics courses.  There are other lists more appropraite for a
question at this level.

(Sorry for a second irrelevant message to the list.  I tried
unsuccessfully to respond privately.)

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] S-Plus?
messages.body_plain:
Not meaning to start a religious war but what are people's feelings
about using S-Plus with beginners?  I see it as a possible alternative
to one of the GUI interfaces for R.  It might be easy for beginners,
but helps one learn the S language.  After the class is over they
could buy S-Plus or use R.  (Yes, I know the student version of S-Plus
is not as easy to access as the free R GUIs.)

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] S-Plus?
messages.body_plain:
I am not familiar with any user interfaces for S-Plus.  I looked at the
insightful website and noted the existence of a student version, but
it appears to be limited to Windows.  That would rule it out for me.

I use R in intro courses, without any GUI.

albyn
